files.complete_text:
LESSON II.

Work and Business.

SECOND WEEK IN FEBRUARY.

THE IDEAL WOMAN.

PROVERBS 31:10-31.

References: Prov. 31:10-31; Eph. 5:22-33; Col. 3:18-25; I Tim. 2:9-15; Titus 2:3-5; I Pet. 3:1-7.

The thirty-first chapter of Proverbs gives us a wonderful picture of the ideal woman. It is a picture that has inspired women through all the ages, and one that is as applicable today as it was when first written. Let us study it carefully, verse by verse, and see what lessons we can learn from it.

"Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies." The word "virtuous" here means much more than moral purity, though that is included. It means a woman of worth, of strength, of capability. Such a woman is rare and precious.

"The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil." Here we see the foundation of a happy home—mutual trust and confidence. The husband can depend upon his wife absolutely. She is worthy of his complete confidence in all things.

"She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life." This is a life-long covenant. Not just when she feels like it, or when things go smoothly, but "all the days of her life" she seeks his welfare.

"She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands." She is industrious and willing. She does not shirk her duties or complain about her work.

"She is like the merchants' ships; she bringeth her food from afar." She is resourceful and far-seeing in providing for her household.

"She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens." She is an early riser and looks after the needs of all under her care.

"She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard." She has business ability and foresight. She can make wise investments.

"She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms." She keeps herself physically fit for her work.

"She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night." She takes pride in the quality of her work and is willing to work late when necessary.

"She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff." She is skilled in the domestic arts.

"She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy." She is charitable and kind to those less fortunate.

"She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet." She provides well for her family and is prepared for all seasons.

"She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple." She dresses well and tastefully, but not extravagantly.

"Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land." Her good management and character reflect credit upon her husband.

"She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant." She has business ability and contributes to the family income.

"Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come." She has character and can face the future with confidence.

"She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness." She speaks wisely and kindly.

"She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness." She is a good manager and is not lazy.

"Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her." She receives the love and appreciation of her family.

"Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all." She stands out among women for her excellence.

"Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the Lord, she shall be praised." True worth comes from character and spirituality, not from outward appearance.

"Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates." She deserves recognition and praise for her accomplishments.

This is indeed a wonderful picture of womanhood. It shows us a woman who is industrious, capable, kind, wise, and God-fearing. She is a good wife, mother, and citizen. She contributes to her community and is respected by all who know her.

Let us strive to be such women ourselves, and to train our daughters to follow this noble example.
files.title: Work and Business
files.notes: Found complete Lesson II on Work and Business in the PDF pages. The file only contained the header, but the full lesson about the ideal woman from Proverbs 31 is visible in the PDF.
